title: Police investigating after man, woman in their 70s found dead in Maine home
author: Audrey Cooney
bullets: Maine State Police are investigating the deaths of Carolyn Harris, 77, and Kevin Harris, 75, in West Bath.
Deputies found the couple dead during a welfare check at their home on State Road, July 13.
Autopsies will determine the cause of death; officials state there is no public danger.

title: Multiple issues after getting an SUV back from the body shop
author: John Paul
bullets: A 2024 Honda CR-V owner is experiencing issues with interior lights and automatic hatch locking post-collision repair.
ADAS recalibration may be needed as programming could be incomplete after the SUV's front-end was repaired.
Contacting the insurance company is advised for potential supplemental claims regarding vehicle programming discrepancies.

title: Officers shot at during Dorchester traffic stop, police say, prompting outcry from union
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Two Boston officers were shot at during a traffic stop in Dorchester; no injuries occurred, but ballistic damage was found.
Rasiel Joel Carbuccia, 20, allegedly fired at officers and was charged with multiple firearm offenses and assault.
The Boston Police Patrolmen’s Association urged officials to condemn the attack and highlighted the dangers officers face.

title: Woman, 25, dead, driver and 3 children hospitalized in crash on I-495 in Andover
author: Audrey Cooney
bullets: A 25-year-old woman from Lawrence died in a crash on I-495 in Andover Wednesday night.
The driver and three children in the minivan were hospitalized; the children's injuries are unknown.
The crash involved a sedan, whose driver was uninjured; the crash is under investigation by State Police.

title: 3 longtime tenants to close or move after Alpha Management buys prominent Allston commercial building
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Alpha Management bought the commercial building at 149-175 Harvard Ave., leading to increased rents and tenant relocations.
The Pet Shop, Ritual Arts, and Regeneration Tattoo will close or move, citing unaffordable rent under the new ownership.
Alpha Management plans to renovate the building, keep existing leases, and relocate some tenants within the property.

title: Paul Pierce, Kevin Garnett to introduce Doc Rivers at Hall of Fame ceremony
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Doc Rivers to be inducted into the Hall of Fame with presenters Paul Pierce and Kevin Garnett.
Rivers coached 27 NBA seasons, achieving 1,194 wins and made two NBA Finals with the Celtics.
The Hall of Fame ceremony will occur on August 15 at Symphony Hall in Springfield.

title: Jayson Tatum says it’s ‘weird’ that Jaylen Brown is no longer a Celtic
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum expressed mixed emotions about Jaylen Brown's trade to the 76ers, calling it 'weird' to face him as a rival.
The Celtics traded Jaylen Brown to Philadelphia for Paul George and multiple draft picks, causing a fan reaction in Boston.
Jaylen Brown comforted a young fan upset by the trade and reflected on feeling 'weird' about moving to the 76ers.

title: How many first-round picks would Drake Maye be worth?
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Drake Maye, at age 23, has been to a Super Bowl, was MVP runner-up, and earned two Pro Bowl selections.
ESPN writer Bill Barnwell suggests Maye's trade value is six first-round picks, highlighting his youth and achievements.
Past NFL QB trades, like Eli Manning and Deshaun Watson, involved significant draft capital, illustrating varied trade values.

title: Furnished island home on Lake Winnipesaukee — can you guess the price?
author: Emily Turner
bullets: A furnished cottage on Lake Winnipesaukee, Gilford, N.H., built in 1952, measures 1,037 square feet with 3 bedrooms.
The island home has lake views, direct lake access, and a private sewer system, with 2025 taxes totaling $8,027.
The listing price for the Lake Winnipesaukee furnished island home is $889,500.

title: ‘The Odyssey’ review: Christopher Nolan defies the odds (and the gods) with a spectacular retelling of Homer’s epic
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Christopher Nolan's 'The Odyssey' offers a human-centric retelling with Matt Damon as Odysseus, highlighting themes of fallibility.
Nolan's adaptation diverges from the original by focusing on Odysseus's humanity rather than the myths of gods and monsters.
The film is noted for stunning IMAX visuals, blending practical effects with a non-linear narrative spanning three distinct timelines.
